what_it_does: Centralizes motion design tokens for a Framer Motion application: spring and eased transition presets, duration constants, a MotionFeel taste toggle, a transition resolver that respects prefers-reduced-motion, and shared fade-rise variants.
observations: Imports only the Transition type from framer-motion.
Exports two spring presets (SPRING_GENTLE, SPRING_SNAPPY) and a material-style EASE_OUT cubic.
DUR groups durations by semantic role: enter, move, exit, colour.
transition(role, feel) maps roles to physics or eased transitions and guards against reduced motion.
Comments cite a design mandate, a no-teleport law, and an S4.3 taste toggle.
British spelling 'colour' is used as a code identifier.
standouts: The reduced-motion guard is baked into the lowest-level transition resolver.
A single function is positioned as the canonical source for all motion transitions.
Only one shared variant (fadeRise) is exported despite the comment describing a broader disclosure grammar.
concerns: Reduced-motion returns duration 0.001, which may still render a frame rather than being truly instant.
transition() reads window.matchMedia at call time, so behavior on the server depends on callers.
The colour role has no spring branch and silently falls through to eased.
fadeRise does not include transitions, leaving timing to each consumer.
EASE_OUT is typed as a raw tuple with no named curve type.
notes: This is the intended single source of truth for motion tokens; consistency depends on components importing these primitives rather than inventing local transitions.
questions: Where is the S4.3 taste toggle configured and how is the feel argument propagated?
Is the 0.001s reduced-motion duration required by Framer Motion, or should it be zero?
Are the peek, open, and dismiss variants of the disclosure grammar defined elsewhere?
How is server-side rendering handled when window.matchMedia is unavailable?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to be the single motion system imported everywhere; the file supplies the primitives and resolver, but adoption must be enforced outside this file.
apparent_themes: Single source of truth for motion.
Taste toggles between spring and eased feels.
Animate only transform and opacity (no teleport).
intention_signals: The file is trying to make motion feel authored and consistent across the product by offering a small, opinionated palette of transitions and a single resolver, while still allowing a user preference between spring and eased feels.
novelty: The explicit 'feel' toggle that swaps spring and eased on the same semantic role is a distinct design choice; otherwise the structure is a conventional motion-token module.
contribution: A centralized transition resolver and a small named set of motion primitives that a fused system could keep as the default animation vocabulary, particularly the reduced-motion guard and role-based transition mapping.
summary_for_embedding: A Framer Motion token module exporting spring/easing presets, duration constants, a MotionFeel type, a role-based transition resolver with reduced-motion support, and shared fade-rise variants.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a single, importable motion system that enforces consistent timing and easing while supporting a spring-or-eased taste toggle and reduced-motion preferences.

symbols.does: Declares the union type of supported motion feels.
symbols.description: A string literal union type 'spring' | 'eased' used by the transition resolver to choose between spring physics and eased curves for a given motion role.

symbols.does: Resolves a Framer Motion Transition for a semantic role and feel, respecting reduced-motion preferences.
symbols.description: Takes a role ('enter' | 'move' | 'exit' | 'colour') and an optional MotionFeel, then returns a Transition. It short-circuits to a near-zero duration when prefers-reduced-motion matches, uses spring presets for enter/move when feel is spring, and otherwise returns an eased duration from the DUR token map.
